tagline: I build production-shaped infrastructure, storage engines, and backend services in Go, Rust, and TypeScript — with a focus on explicit error handling, clean service boundaries, and observable, reliable systems.
about: I'm a self-directed backend and systems engineer based in Nigeria. I've been building the kind of projects you'd encounter in production — authentication systems, persistence layers, distributed workflows, probabilistic data structures, and developer tooling.

My primary languages are Rust and Go, with TypeScript for edge and serverless work. I care about how systems fail, how they recover, and how engineers on-call understand what's happening. Structured logging, request IDs, and latency tracking are not afterthoughts in my code.
Actively seeking backend, systems, or infrastructure roles — internship or entry-level, remote or international.

projects.name: CrispyDB
projects.desc: In-memory BTreeMap-backed key-value storage engine with async background persistence. Read paths are accelerated by Bloom filters (xxh3_128) to skip unnecessary disk I/O. Configurable builder pattern, tombstone-based deletes, and a manifest tracking flushed file numbers across shutdown cycles.

projects.name: Redir
projects.desc: Layered Gin service with strict package separation across routes, handlers, middleware, store, cache, and repository. Full auth stack: bcrypt, Redis-backed sessions, email verification, Google OAuth. Multipart uploads via AWS SDK v2, presigned URL caching, per-access metadata capture, and route-scoped rate limiting.

projects.name: dnsgo
projects.desc: Dual-mode DNS server: authoritative for its own zone, recursive resolver for everything else — forwarding to root servers and walking the full delegation chain. Clean package separation across cmd, internal, and configs; wire-protocol handled by a proven DNS library so focus stays on resolver and zone logic.

projects.name: ShipFlow
projects.desc: Collaboration platform with workspace membership, RBAC, and task management. GitHub App integration with HMAC webhook verification, PR tracking, and cleanup flows. Cloudflare Worker (Hono + D1 + Drizzle + CF Queues) handles async webhook ingestion and an AI review pipeline, sharing event contracts and idempotency keys with the backend. This is currently a private repo and will be made available on request

alsoBuilding.name: bolt
alsoBuilding.desc: async order processing via Redis Streams, invoice generation, webhook delivery

alsoBuilding.name: watch
alsoBuilding.desc: nodemon-style file watcher that re-runs a configured command on directory changes

availability: Open to backend, systems, or infrastructure roles — internship or entry-level, remote or international.
